problem_addressed: Standard self-attention lacks explicit temporal dynamics, with tokens attending independently based on static feature similarity rather than consistent state evolution over time.
core_claim: Self-attention can be reformulated as precision-weighted state estimation under a learned linear stochastic dynamical prior, where attention weights are determined by consistency with probabilistic state predictions.
key_contributions: Introduction of Robust Filter Attention (RFA) as a formulation of self-attention as robust state estimation
Development of Spectrally Coupled RFA (SC-RFA) to address phase wrap-around in long-range extrapolation
Dynamical interpretation of positional mechanisms connecting rotational embeddings to transport and uncertainty propagation
novelty: Treats tokens as noisy observations of a latent trajectory governed by linear stochastic differential equations, with attention weights determined by consistency under this model rather than static feature similarity, unlike deterministic geometric approaches (RoPE) or monotonic recency biasing (ALiBi).
method_summary: RFA models each token as a noisy observation of a latent trajectory governed by a linear stochastic differential equation. Past keys are propagated to the query position under learned dynamics, with attention weights computed from prediction errors measured under precisions determined by the Differential Lyapunov Equation (DLE).
method_components: Linear stochastic differential equation dynamics for state propagation
Differential Lyapunov Equation (DLE) for precision calculation
Power-law robust weighting for attention computation
Spectrally coupled decay rates in SC-RFA variant
key_innovation: Attention as precision-weighted state estimation under a stochastic dynamical prior, coupling transport and uncertainty propagation in a principled way where tokens are weighted according to their consistency with learned dynamics.
assumptions: Linear stochastic dynamics for state evolution
theoretical_grounding: Grounded in stochastic process theory, specifically linear stochastic differential equations and the Differential Lyapunov Equation for uncertainty propagation. The paper provides derivations showing how RFA reduces to standard attention under special cases and connects to existing positional mechanisms.
stated_limitations: Reliance on isotropic noise and decay assumptions for computational complexity matching
unstated_weaknesses: Potential sensitivity to hyperparameters in SDE formulation
Assumption of linear SDEs might limit expressiveness
threats_to_validity: Computational complexity claims under specific assumptions
Limited evaluation to language modeling benchmarks
reproducibility: medium (code availability not mentioned in abstract)
open_questions: How does RFA perform on tasks other than language modeling?
What are the optimal hyperparameters for the SDE formulation?
future_work: Further exploration of dynamical interpretation of positional embeddings
gaps_this_enables: Application of state estimation techniques to attention mechanisms
relevance_to_topic: Directly relevant as it proposes an efficient attention mechanism with matching computational complexity
limitations_stated: Reliance on isotropic noise and decay assumptions for computational complexity matching
title: Robust Filter Attention: Self-Attention as Precision-Weighted State Estimation
